stories.title: Maya's Morning Loop
stories.text: Maya boils the kettle, clips on Woofa's lead, and calls it her 'soft reset'. On the way back, she picks lemons and promises: lemonade later, stargazing tonight.

stories.title: Leo's Weekend Special
stories.text: Leo flips something golden in the frying pans like it's a sport. He announces 'Backyard BBQ ready' even when it's just breakfast — confidence is his secret ingredient.

stories.title: Mission: Snack to Space
stories.text: Finn claims the solar panel is powering his 'rocket snacks'. He builds an oat bar launchpad and asks if the kitchen microwave can do 'warp speed'.

stories.title: Laura's Backyard Session
stories.text: Laura sets up the cricket stumps like a mini stadium. After practice she sits on the garden bench, tapping drum rhythms on her knees like it's a victory parade.

stories.title: Olive, The Tiny Hype Queen
stories.text: Olive claps at the fruit bowl like it's a concert. Whiskers pretends not to care, Woofa begs for snacks anyway — Olive calls this 'teamwork'.

stories.title: Joy's Home Ops System
stories.text: Joy runs the house like a calm operations lead: groceries sorted, first aid checked, cleaning rota invisible but flawless. Everyone swears it's magic. Joy calls it 'process'.

stories.title: Woofa's Garden Patrol
stories.text: Woofa circles the picket fence like a tiny security guard. He sniffs the compost bin, decides it's 'suspicious', and reports back with one triumphant bark.

stories.title: Whiskers' Royal Decree
stories.text: Whiskers ignores the toys until everyone is watching — then pounces like it's a show. Later she sits under the palm tree shade and approves the household with a slow blink.

stories.title: Zap, zap! Dinner's ready!
stories.text: The microwave beeps twice and suddenly the whole house has an opinion. Plates appear like magic — even Whiskers supervises from a distance.

stories.title: Tea or coffee?
stories.text: Kettle versus coffee maker: the friendliest rivalry in the kitchen. Maya calls it 'morning diplomacy' — Leo calls it 'fuel'.

stories.title: Cappuccino coming up!
stories.text: Maya watches the coffee maker like it's a tiny science experiment. Finn insists the foam looks like a cloud from outer space.

stories.title: Where's the Vegemite?
stories.text: Toast pops up like a surprise. Someone yells 'Vegemite!' like it's a treasure hunt, and suddenly breakfast turns into a game.

stories.title: Time to set the table
stories.text: Joy teaches the kids a table-setting trick: forks don't have to match to look nice. 'Home is allowed to be imperfect,' she says, and everyone believes her.

stories.title: Fried, poached or scrambled?
stories.text: Leo turns eggs into a choose-your-own-adventure story. Finn votes scrambled, Laura votes 'whatever is fastest', and Olive votes by clapping.

stories.title: An apple a day...
stories.text: The fruit bowl becomes a stage for Maya's 'fruit platter perfection' moment. Finn tries to name each piece like a planet: Strawberry is definitely Mars.

stories.title: Nutritious & delicious
stories.text: Fresh veggies meet frozen veggies: teamwork, not competition. Joy calls it 'backup strategy', Leo calls it 'dinner insurance'.

stories.title: Homegrown goodness
stories.text: Woofa discovers the carrot patch and decides it's his new hangout spot. Maya laughs: 'He's gardening. Emotionally.'

stories.title: Flower power!
stories.text: Maya rearranges the plants like she's curating a tiny gallery. Laura says it's 'a vibe'. Finn says it's 'alien flora'.

stories.title: Every house needs these
stories.text: Joy labels the grocery bags and checks the first aid kit like a pro. The kids think it's boring — until someone needs a band-aid. Then Joy is a hero.

stories.title: Gather round the garden table
stories.text: The garden bench becomes the 'lemonade meeting spot'. Maya tells one short story, Finn adds three plot twists, and suddenly everyone is laughing.

stories.title: Backyard BBQ ready
stories.text: The garden table appears and Leo instantly announces BBQ mode. Laura sets the cricket stumps nearby — because in this house, sport and food are best friends.

stories.title: Watch out for Woofa!
stories.text: Woofa patrols the picket fence like he owns the whole suburb. Whiskers watches from afar, silently judging his 'security technique'.

stories.title: Let's make lemonade
stories.text: Kettle boils, lemons appear, and suddenly it's a lemonade lab. Finn asks if lemonade can power rockets. Joy says: 'Try it and write notes.'

stories.title: Cool off in the shade
stories.text: Palm tree shade + solar panel = Maya's dream: chill and sustainable. Leo says it's 'a holiday corner' even if they never leave the backyard.

stories.title: Fruit platter perfection
stories.text: Strawberries join the fruit bowl like the final sparkle on a crown. Whiskers inspects the arrangement and approves by not knocking it over (rare).

stories.title: Play time!
stories.text: Finn narrates a full movie plot using the toys as actors. Laura adds drum beats on the table. Olive claps. The audience is… mostly Woofa.

stories.title: Ready to recycle
stories.text: Recycling becomes a tiny family ritual. Laura says saving the planet is part of her cricket training. Nobody argues because it somehow makes sense.

stories.title: Good for the garden
stories.text: Compost bin arrives and suddenly the garden feels 'serious'. Joy calls it circular thinking. Finn calls it 'the worm clubhouse'.

stories.title: Cleaner, greener energy
stories.text: Maya loves the solar panel like it's a collectible superpower. She points at the sky and says: 'That's our charger.' Finn is impressed.

stories.title: Howzat!
stories.text: Laura sets up the cricket set and turns the backyard into a stadium. Leo does commentary. Maya does the scoreboard. Gender roles? Not invited.

stories.title: Can we bake cupcakes?
stories.text: Finn asks Joy for cupcakes again. Joy hands him the measuring job like it's a promotion. Finn takes it very seriously.

stories.title: Sambos for school
stories.text: Bread + toaster = school morning solved. Laura packs her lunch like an athlete prepping for game day — efficient, proud, unstoppable.

stories.title: Household essentials
stories.text: Joy restocks essentials without making it a big deal. The house feels calmer. Maya says: 'That's the real luxury.'

stories.title: Sparkle & shine
stories.text: Leo puts on 'cleaning music' and calls it the clean squad. Everyone laughs — then accidentally cleans half the house because the beat is good.

stories.title: What's for dinner?
stories.text: Meat shows up and Leo starts planning dinner like it's a festival lineup. Maya negotiates for more veggies. Finn demands a 'space-themed' dessert.

stories.title: Eat your greens
stories.text: Frozen veggies meet microwave: a fast, honest partnership. Joy says: 'Some days survival is the recipe.' Everyone nods.

stories.title: Stock up the pantry
stories.text: Groceries arrive, bags get sorted, and the pantry looks like a tiny store. Finn pretends to be cashier. Whiskers pretends she owns the shop.

stories.title: Start the day strong
stories.text: Breakfast bowl + fruit bowl = morning wins. Laura eats like she's fueling training. Maya calls it 'gentle strength' and smiles.

stories.title: Time to feed the pets
stories.text: The pet food appears and Whiskers suddenly remembers everyone's name. Woofa sits perfectly still. It's the most peaceful moment of the day.

stories.title: Snack time!
stories.text: Oats become Finn's 'mission snack' again. Joy suggests a recipe, Leo suggests a topping, and Maya suggests taking it outside for stargazing later.

stories.title: Backyard BBQ & Cricket Day
stories.text: Leo runs the BBQ like a chef on TV. Laura calls the shots on the cricket pitch. Maya keeps score, Joy cheers, and Finn adds dramatic commentary like it's the finals.

stories.title: The Lemonade Garden Club
stories.text: Maya hosts a 'garden club' on the bench — membership: whoever shows up. Lemonade is mandatory, jokes are encouraged, and Woofa is honorary security.

stories.title: Cupcake Lab: Approved
stories.text: Finn becomes the official taste-tester, Joy becomes the calm instructor, and the coffee maker becomes the lab machine. Leo pretends he's 'just passing by' for a sample.

stories.title: Sparkle & Shine Playlist Party
stories.text: Leo puts on music, Maya turns cleaning into a tiny dance session. Joy approves silently because the house gets cleaner without anyone feeling forced.

stories.title: Pets Rule the Castle
stories.text: Woofa guards the fence like a loyal knight. Whiskers rules the kingdom like a queen. Everyone else? Happily employed as snack suppliers.

stories.title: Green Home Week
stories.text: Joy declares 'Green Home Week' like it's a fun challenge. Recycling, composting, solar — suddenly the whole house feels proud of small habits.

stories.title: Breakfast Power Combo
stories.text: Toast, oats, and a breakfast bowl — the morning lineup is complete. Laura says it's 'training fuel', Maya says it's 'gentle comfort', Finn says it's 'rocket energy'.

stories.title: Movie Night, Quiet Win
stories.text: Microwave snacks, fruit on the side, toys scattered like tiny props. Whiskers curls up and the house finally goes quiet — the kind of quiet that feels safe.
